cards.title: New task
cards.description:
Test[adrian3/Ade-s-design-system](https://github.com/adrian3/Ade-s-design-system)

Apply the design system from this repo to this project.

Design system source of truth:
- ./design.md
- ./baseline.css
- ./tailwind.theme.json
- ./index.html
- ./baseline.html

Your job:
- Restyle the existing app so it conforms to this design system.
- Remove as much existing styling as possible unless it is structurally necessary.
- Prefer deleting old CSS, component-library overrides, theme files, utility clutter, and inline styles rather than layering new styles on top.
- Make the final result feel like one coherent system, not a mix of the old app and the new design system.

Important design constraints:
- Editorial, prose-first, minimal visual language
- White main canvas
- `#fafafa` for header/footer/supporting surfaces
- Vollkorn for reading/display text
- Montserrat for utility text and buttons
- Single accent color: `#cc3300`
- Tight 4px control radius
- No gradients
- No dark sections unless absolutely required for functionality
- Minimal chrome, light borders, whitespace-first hierarchy
- Buttons, inputs, and selects should share consistent control height
- Heroicons are the preferred icon set

Implementation priorities:
1. Read and follow `design.md` first.
2. Reuse tokens from `tailwind.theme.json` if the project uses Tailwind.
3. If the project renders raw HTML or markdown, use the ideas in `baseline.css`.
4. Remove conflicting old styling systems where possible.
5. Simplify components instead of preserving decorative legacy patterns.
6. Keep accessibility intact.

What to preserve:
- Existing functionality
- Existing content and information architecture unless styling changes require small structural adjustments
- Responsive behavior
- Accessibility and usable contrast
- Important product logic

What to avoid:
- Do not invent a new visual direction.
- Do not keep legacy brand colors, gradients, shadows, or radii unless I explicitly ask.
- Do not leave old styling in place just because it works.
- Do not create a hybrid of the old design and the new system.

Deliverables:
- Update the code directly.
- Briefly summarize what old styling systems or files were removed or reduced.
- Point out any places where the app structure fought the design system and what you changed to resolve that.
